verses.1: After this Job opened his mouth, and cursed the day of his birth.
verses.3: “Let the day perish in which I was born,
verses.5: Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it for their own.
verses.6: As for that night, let thick darkness seize on it.
verses.7: Behold, let that night be barren.
verses.8: Let them curse it who curse the day,
verses.9: Let the stars of its twilight be dark.
verses.10: because it didn’t shut up the doors of my mother’s womb,
verses.11: “Why didn’t I die from the womb?
verses.12: Why did the knees receive me?
verses.13: For now I should have lain down and been quiet.
verses.14: with kings and counselors of the earth,
verses.15: or with princes who had gold,
verses.16: or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been,
verses.17: There the wicked cease from troubling.
verses.18: There the prisoners are at ease together.
verses.19: The small and the great are there.
verses.20: “Why is light given to him who is in misery,
verses.21: who long for death, but it doesn’t come;
verses.23: Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden,
verses.24: For my sighing comes before I eat.
verses.25: For the thing which I fear comes on me,
verses.26: I am not at ease, neither am I quiet, neither do I have rest;
